ground_truth.title: Exploring the AndroidX WebGPU API in Kotlin
ground_truth.main_content:
Setup: Add the latest package of the androidx.webgpu release to the app module's build.gradle.kts with dependencies implementation("androidx.webgpu:webgpu:1.0.0-alpha01").

Get GPU Device Info: To start exploring the WebGPU API, write a method that retrieves the details of the underlying GPU device. The WebGPU adapter (of type GPUAdapter) identifies an underlying GPUDevice present in the system. The GPUAdapter can be used to request a GPUDevice, get information about the device i.e. get GPUAdapterInfo or access the features and limits of the device.

Writing a Compute Shader: To execute a program on the GPU with the WebGPU API, write the program in WGSL (WebGPU Shader Language). WGSL is a high-level language used to express parallel computation by defined typed variables, routines and common Math functions. The shader operates on a collection of vectors stored as a flat array and computes a symmetric similarity matrix. Each GPU thread (a total of 16 * 16 = 256 parallel threads) processes one pair of vectors identified by 2D coordinates (i, j), but only computes the upper triangle of the matrix to avoid redundant work since cosine similarity is symmetric.

GPU Buffers: Execution on a GPU device requires memory regions to store inputs and outputs of the program. These memory regions are represented with a GPUBuffer. We load data into a GPUBuffer, the GPU performs the computation using the data and writes the outputs to another GPUBuffer. A UNIFORM buffer is ideal to store parameters required for the shader. It has a smaller capacity and is read-only for the shader.

GPU Bind Groups: To associate buffers with the shader, define a GPUBindGroup, where the visibility of each buffer, its type, and index within the group are mentioned. A visibility of ShaderStage.Compute ensures that the bind group entry is only visible to compute shaders.

GPU Compute Pipeline and Command Encoder: A GPUComputePipeline represents the sequence of steps performed by a compute shader module. The pipeline is defined with a GPUPipelineLayout that accepts a list of GPUBindGroupLayout.

Mapping a Buffer: GPUQueue i.e. device.queue.submit schedules the pipeline for execution on the GPU device. To read the results, use the resultsBuffer.mapAsync method that informs when the contents of resultsBuffer have been mapped i.e. ready for use.
with: Add the latest package of the androidx.webgpu release to the app module's build.gradle.kts: dependencies { implementation("androidx.webgpu:webgpu:1.0.0-alpha01") }

The WebGPU adapter (of type GPUAdapter) identifies an underlying GPUDevice present in the system. The GPUAdapter can be used to request a GPUDevice, get information about the device i.e. get GPUAdapterInfo or access the features and limits of the device.

To execute a program on the GPU with the WebGPU API, we need to write the program in WGSL (WebGPU Shader Language). WGSL is a high-level language used to express parallel computation by defined typed variables, routines and common Math functions.

Execution on a GPU device requires memory regions to store inputs and outputs of the program. These memory regions are represented with a GPUBuffer. We load data into a GPUBuffer, the GPU performs the computation using the data and writes the outputs to another GPUBuffer.

To associate these buffers with the shader, we define a GPUBindGroup, where the visibility of each buffer, its type, and index within the group are mentioned. A visibility of ShaderStage.Compute ensures that the bind group entry is only visible to compute shaders.
